bio: Building and rebuilding data organizations is what I do best. I step into environments where the data function is broken, immature, or non-existent, and turn it into one that actually delivers. Across healthcare, fintech, ed-tech, ad-tech, and medical devices, the pattern has been consistent: assess what's there, design what's needed, build the team and the infrastructure, and deliver measurable business outcomes. My background in industrial engineering and Six Sigma gives me a process discipline that's rare in analytics leadership. I think about data infrastructure the way an engineer thinks about systems — reliability, scalability, and measurable performance at every layer. I'm also deeply technical. I can write the dbt models, architect the warehouse, and lead the governance board in the same week.
